item.title: Bottom shelf employeed skibidi toilet memes
item.description:
Do you collect Imgur notifications? Well, have I got news for you! You no longer have to wait for the posts to hit "most viral" to be notified. Now, you can be notified as soon as I post, so you can catch the steaming hot shitposts fresh out the skibidi toilet, or whatever. The way god intended.
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
Join for the games. Shit, man. We play everything...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving degen there to play it with you. Join for the memes. We share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the NSFW memes. Join to ping @humlyhums Join to meet @crimebrulee the biggest problem in the server. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka
You made us drink Enchilada soda. >:( twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka Come heckle us. Follow the channel to be notified when we go live.
Throwback to a baby Taxi Bear snuggling with old man Nutterbutt for adorable #sleeperagent tax
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: Neil the Seal
item.description: Neil (born October 2020), also known as Neil the Seal, is a southern elephant seal in the Australian state of Tasmania. He was born in Salem Bay, Tasmania. Neil gained fame in July 2022 after resting in Hobart for his month-long moulting period. While most people kept a safe distance, incidents of humans disturbing the seal led to traffic cones placed to protect him. Neil, playing with the traffic cones, attracted more attention to his unusual behavior. Later events saw Neil more closely interacting with humans, often leaving the beach and resting on the city's roads, or even following people. In one instance in 2023, Neil was found basking in someone's front lawn for a few hours, while another instance downing a fence in Dunalley. He was relocated in April 2023 after people and dogs harassed him in Kingston Beach. Neil is an internet celebrity, having at least 126,000 followers on Instagram under the username "neiltheseal22" as of January 20, 2024, and over 730,000 followers on TikTok as of January 3, 2024. He is being monitored by the Marine Conservation Program, established by Tasmania's Department of Natural Resources and Environment, for health and safety reasons. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neil_(seal)
item.dc:creator: l0calh0st

item.title: Jasmine is out of surgery!!
item.description:
Got this pic from the foster coordinator at the shelter. Jasmine is out of surgery and it went well! Look at that little baby. Such a trooper
She’s gonna get some good pain meds, and I am not planning on going anywhere tonight so she’ll have a warm lap to snooze and recover on. I can’t wait to go pick her up!! (Update #1: https://imgur.com/gallery/FCrBTuw ) (Update #2: https://imgur.com/gallery/1h76Fy9 )
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Some funnies in exchange for some help
item.description: I'm hoping this one goes through. I'm trying to get a little help with an urgent bill as well as some dog food and flea meds for my dog, Bandit. I am also hoping to sell some art to make ends meet but lack the proper places to advertise. Ill probably make another post with the art, or I can put links in a comment. Edit: wow most viral! That's amazing haha. I put the link for above in my bio if anyone wants to help.

item.title: Hey, Imgur! I had a stroke!
item.description: Long time no post, Imgur!! Last time I posted anything it was that I’d lost a crap ton of weight after getting sober (at age 32)— now I’m here to send out a PSA about continuing care… I’ve been sober for 5 years now and have lost a collective 217 lbs. I had a Panniculectomy (removal of excess skin from the abdomen) in December of 2023. On May 6th of 2024, I had a stroke at the age of 34. I changed absolutely everything about myself and my lifestyle and thought I was in the clear. I didn’t take proper care of myself after surgery, began exercising too soon, and continued working 16-18 hour days. My brain kicked my ass for it. #addiction doesn’t care who you are, your gender, race, orientation, how much money you make, how you were raised, if you like bananas or not— it picks its victims at random and it will destroy everyone and everything you love before finally coming for your life. While in the hospital it was suggested that I try ECT (electroconvulsive therapy, aka electric shock therapy) or TMS (transcranial magnetic stimulation). I opted for ECT. I researched and read reviews for almost a week before I picked, and I am SO GLAD I CHOSE ECT. I did 12 rounds: the first 4 at 30% and the remaining 8 at 60%. I won’t lie— it was very painful at first. When I woke up after the first treatment, I could barely speak, open my jaw more than half an inch, and each time I tried to shift my body, every muscle inside of me screeched like a howler monkey in heat. Over the course of 7 weeks, the doctors, nurses, and techs learned how to best manage me before and after the sessions and I was taken care of with so, so much support. Thank you #mayoclinic It completely changed my life, guys. I began therapy for depression when I was 12 and I have never felt like this in my adult life. After the stroke I lost my job and, subsequently, the house I had worked so hard to buy. I’m happy to say that my lovely sister took me in, has helped me make it to all of my physical therapy appointments, and I begin a new job after Labor Day! I still have numbness in parts of the right side of my body and sometimes can’t help but limp, but I am one of the fortunate ones that is able to say she survived. I’ll always be grateful for this community and I apologize for the selfies. I never want anyone to ever have to go through what I did to myself and my family. I’ve missed Imgur so much!
item.dc:creator: GhostRadventures
